pattern: A fixed-width window has only one shape to check, so a single pass comparing the two ends against the middle is enough.
hints: Every window you need to look at has exactly three elements, so there is no need to track a running sum or any data structure -- just slide a window of size 3 across the array.
For a window (a, b, c), the condition is that a + c equals half of b -- to avoid any issue with b being odd, compare 2*(a+c) against b instead of dividing.
Walk i from 0 to len(nums)-3 and test nums[i], nums[i+1], nums[i+2] directly, counting how many times the doubled-sum condition holds.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, one qualifying window among three
statement example, the only window does not qualify
shortest possible array, exactly one window
middle value is odd, so a naive integer-division check would misfire without the doubled comparison
negative numbers whose sum still satisfies the condition
extreme constraint values at both ends of the allowed range
several overlapping windows, only some qualifying

stress.description: random arrays across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
